sources.exact_document_title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease
sources.population: Adults and children at risk of or living with CKD, with age, cause, GFR, albuminuria, progression, treatment, and referral qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: CKD detection, evaluation, monitoring, medication stewardship, referral, and longitudinal kidney care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for selected CKD, GFR, albuminuria, medication-review, and referral documentation. Diagnosis, treatment, medicine changes or doses, fixed monitoring intervals beyond stated qualifiers, and UAE service pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_sections.heading: Detection of CKD and evaluation of chronicity
exact_sections.locator: Practice Points 1.1.1.1–1.1.1.2 and 1.1.3.1–1.1.3.2; summary PDF pages 34 and 54–57
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports GFR and urine-albumin testing, repeat confirmation, prior GFR or albuminuria trends, imaging, history, and the minimum chronicity context without diagnosing CKD from one result.

exact_sections.heading: Evaluation of cause and kidney-function context
exact_sections.locator: Practice Point 1.1.4.1, Figure 8, Table 6, and Practice Point 1.2.1.1; summary PDF pages 34–35 and 61
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports careful medical, family, medicine, urinary, systemic, examination, urinalysis, ACR, electrolyte, and imaging context in clinician-led CKD evaluation.

exact_sections.heading: Evaluation of albuminuria
exact_sections.locator: Practice Points 1.3.1.1–1.3.1.3 and Table 16; summary PDF pages 39 and 75–76
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports ACR, PCR, urinalysis, quantitative confirmation, and interpretation factors including haematuria, menstruation, exercise, and infection.

exact_sections.heading: Monitoring GFR and albuminuria in CKD
exact_sections.locator: Practice Points 2.1.1–2.1.5; summary PDF pages 40 and 81–82
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

exact_sections.heading: Polypharmacy and drug stewardship
exact_sections.locator: Practice Point 4.3.1 and Figure 46; summary PDF pages 50 and 135–137
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports periodic medication review, accurate medication lists, adherence, continued indication, interaction, monitoring, reconciliation, and nephrotoxic-medicine context without treatment changes or doses.

exact_sections.heading: Referral to specialist kidney care services
exact_sections.locator: Practice Point 5.1.1 and Figure 48; summary PDF pages 51 and 140–141
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led referral documentation for qualified sustained GFR decline, significant albuminuria with haematuria, refractory hypertension, persistent potassium abnormalities, and other listed circumstances.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Medicines adherence: involving patients in decisions about prescribed medicines and supporting adherence
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
sources.population: People prescribed medicines and healthcare professionals who prescribe, dispense, or review medicines.
sources.clinical_setting: Shared medicine decisions, adherence assessment, support, and periodic medicine review.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for non-judgemental adherence history, missed-dose or medicine-taking behaviour documentation, beliefs, concerns, practical barriers, preferred support, and periodic review. It does not recommend a medicine, dose, change, or adherence intervention automatically.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.19–1.1.23 — knowledge, beliefs, and concerns about medicines
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting the person’s understanding, perceived need, concerns, adverse-effect concerns, daily-routine fit, and questions during medicine review.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.8 — non-judgemental adherence assessment and tailored support
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting missed doses, stopping or restarting, intentional or practical barriers, and support preferences without generating an intervention.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.2 — periodic review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting periodic review of knowledge, concerns, need, and clinician prescribing decisions.
